title: Footprints Without Feet – Concept Mastery Helper
author: H.G. Wells

concepts.lesson: The prose explains how Griffin discovers a way to make the human body invisible through scientific experiments, showing the power of human intellect and innovation.
concepts.real_example: Modern science has developed technologies like camouflage and stealth to reduce visibility.

concepts.lesson: Griffin misuses his scientific discovery for selfish and criminal purposes, proving that intelligence without morality leads to destruction.
concepts.real_example: Talented individuals misusing skills for illegal activities.

concepts.lesson: Griffin’s strange appearance and behaviour make others view him as eccentric, highlighting how society reacts to unusual individuals.
concepts.real_example: People often judge others based on appearance and behaviour.

concepts.lesson: The story clearly shows that scientific achievements must be guided by ethics. Griffin’s lack of responsibility turns a great discovery into a curse.
concepts.real_example: Unethical scientific experiments causing harm to society.

concepts.lesson: The villagers’ fear reflects how humans react to unexplained phenomena. Invisibility creates panic because it challenges normal understanding.
concepts.real_example: Fear caused by unexplained natural or scientific events.

concepts.lesson: Griffin’s invisibility leads to physical and emotional isolation, showing that separation from society can damage mental stability.
Invisibility gives Griffin power, but his inability to handle it responsibly turns him violent and destructive.
concepts.real_example: People misusing authority or power when unaccountable.

concepts.lesson: Griffin’s behaviour shows signs of mental instability caused by isolation, fear, and constant conflict with society.
concepts.real_example: Mental stress leading to aggressive behaviour.
concepts.source_section: Middle to end of the prose

concepts.lesson: H.G. Wells uses science fiction to warn readers about the dangers of uncontrolled scientific ambition.
concepts.real_example: Science fiction predicting real-world ethical problems.

concepts.lesson: The prose emphasizes that scientists must be accountable for how their discoveries affect humanity.
concepts.real_example: Ethical guidelines in modern scientific research.
